rules.name: naming-dag-title-case-edit
rules.evidence: Write **Dag** (title case) in all prose. Keep the all-caps or lowercase spelling only when reproducing a literal code token — never rewrite these, even inside fenced code blocks

rules.name: naming-dag-title-case-write
rules.evidence: Write **Dag** (title case) in all prose. Keep the all-caps or lowercase spelling only when reproducing a literal code token — never rewrite these, even inside fenced code blocks

rules.name: no-direct-pytest-python-airflow-on-host
rules.evidence: Never run pytest, python, or airflow commands directly on the host — always use breeze.

rules.name: ruff-after-python-edit
rules.evidence: Always format and check Python files with ruff immediately after writing or editing them: `uv run ruff format <file_path>` and `uv run ruff check --fix <file_path>`. Do this for every Python file you create or modify, before moving on to the next step.

rules.name: ruff-after-python-write
rules.evidence: Always format and check Python files with ruff immediately after writing or editing them: `uv run ruff format <file_path>` and `uv run ruff check --fix <file_path>`. Do this for every Python file you create or modify, before moving on to the next step.

rules.name: no-assert-in-production-code-edit
rules.evidence: No `assert` in production code.

rules.name: no-assert-in-production-code-write
rules.evidence: No `assert` in production code.

rules.name: time-monotonic-not-time-edit
rules.evidence: `time.monotonic()` for durations, not `time.time()`.

rules.name: time-monotonic-not-time-write
rules.evidence: `time.monotonic()` for durations, not `time.time()`.

rules.name: no-session-commit-airflow-core-edit
rules.evidence: In `airflow-core`, functions with a `session` parameter must not call `session.commit()`. Use keyword-only `session` parameters.

rules.name: no-session-commit-airflow-core-write
rules.evidence: In `airflow-core`, functions with a `session` parameter must not call `session.commit()`. Use keyword-only `session` parameters.

rules.name: no-new-raise-airflowexception-edit
rules.evidence: Never add new direct `raise AirflowException(...)` usages — the community is actively reducing them, not adding more, and the `check-no-new-airflow-exceptions` prek hook enforces this across `airflow-core`, `airflow-ctl`, `task-sdk`, `providers`, and `shared`.

rules.name: no-new-raise-airflowexception-write
rules.evidence: Never add new direct `raise AirflowException(...)` usages — the community is actively reducing them, not adding more, and the `check-no-new-airflow-exceptions` prek hook enforces this across `airflow-core`, `airflow-ctl`, `task-sdk`, `providers`, and `shared`.

rules.name: apache-license-header-new-files
rules.evidence: Apache License header on all new files (prek enforces this).

rules.name: newsfragment-only-when-user-facing
rules.evidence: Golden rule: never create a newsfragment unless you are certain the change is user-facing. If you are not sure the change is visible to users ... do not add one.

rules.name: no-newsfragments-providers-airflowctl
rules.evidence: Never add newsfragments for `providers/` or `airflow-ctl/` — those distributions are released from `main` and their release managers regenerate the changelog from `git log`, so per-PR newsfragments are not consumed.

rules.name: no-self-co-authored-by
rules.evidence: NEVER add Co-Authored-By with yourself as co-author of the commit. Agents cannot be authors, humans can be, Agents are assistants.

rules.name: commit-message-no-conventional-commits
rules.evidence: Use the imperative mood and a plain message — do not use Conventional Commits prefixes (`fix:`, `feat:`, `chore:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, …). apache/airflow does not follow that convention.

rules.name: pr-title-no-conventional-commits
rules.evidence: The same rule applies to PR titles. (do not use Conventional Commits prefixes)

rules.name: prek-install-before-commit
rules.evidence: Always run `prek install` before committing any code. It installs the commit-msg hook (in addition to pre-commit) so the Conventional Commits guard runs locally.

rules.name: before-push-self-review-and-rebase
rules.evidence: Before pushing, perform a self-review of your changes ... 1. Review the full diff ... 2. Read code-review.instructions.md ... 4. Run regular (fast) static checks ... 6. Run relevant individual tests ... Before pushing, always rebase your branch onto the latest target branch (usually main) to avoid merge conflicts.

rules.name: never-push-to-upstream-or-main
rules.evidence: Always push branches to origin. Never push directly to upstream (and never push directly to main on either remote).

rules.name: verify-remote-naming-before-remote-command
rules.evidence: Before running any remote-based command, run `git remote -v` and verify the names match this convention.

rules.name: check-existing-pr-before-creating
rules.evidence: Before working on an issue, check for open PRs already addressing it (`gh pr list --search "<issue number or keywords>"`, and look for `closes:` / `fixes:` references).

rules.name: pr-not-issue-when-fix-imminent
rules.evidence: If you already know how to fix the problem and you (or the user) are going to open the PR shortly, do not file a GitHub issue first.

rules.name: no-tagging-individuals-in-gh-comments
rules.evidence: AI agents MUST NOT mention or tag individual contributors, committers, PMC members, or maintainers using GitHub usernames (e.g. `@user`) unless explicitly instructed by a human reviewer.

rules.name: attribution-footer-on-gh-comments
rules.evidence: Anything an agent drafts that ends up posted to GitHub on the user's account — PR / issue comments, PR-level reviews, line-level review comments, discussion replies — must end with an attribution footer.

rules.name: never-commit-secrets
rules.evidence: Boundaries — Never: Commit secrets, credentials, or tokens.

rules.name: never-hand-edit-uv-lock
rules.evidence: The `uv.lock` file is generated by `uv lock`, `uv sync` and is committed to the repo ... If at any point in time you have a conflict with `uv.lock`, simply delete it and run `uv lock` to regenerate it. (Boundaries — Never: Edit generated files by hand when a generation workflow exists.)

rules.name: no-destructive-git-ops-unless-requested
rules.evidence: Boundaries — Never: Use destructive git operations unless explicitly requested.

rules.name: ask-first-new-dependency-edit
rules.evidence: Boundaries — Ask first: New dependencies with broad impact.

rules.name: ask-first-new-dependency-write
rules.evidence: Boundaries — Ask first: New dependencies with broad impact.

rules.name: pytest-not-unittest-testcase-edit
rules.evidence: Use pytest patterns, not `unittest.TestCase`.

rules.name: pytest-not-unittest-testcase-write
rules.evidence: Use pytest patterns, not `unittest.TestCase`.

rules.name: no-datetime-now-in-tests-edit
rules.evidence: Use `time_machine` for time-dependent tests. Do not use `datetime.now()`

rules.name: no-datetime-now-in-tests-write
rules.evidence: Use `time_machine` for time-dependent tests. Do not use `datetime.now()`

rules.name: no-caplog-text-assertions-edit
rules.evidence: Do not assert on raw log text (`caplog.text` for example), these are legacy string matching APIs planned for removal.

rules.name: no-caplog-text-assertions-write
rules.evidence: Do not assert on raw log text (`caplog.text` for example), these are legacy string matching APIs planned for removal.

rules.name: keep-selective-checks-doc-in-sync
rules.evidence: Keep selective-checks behaviour and its documentation in sync. ... update `dev/breeze/doc/ci/04_selective_checks.md` in the same PR ... and add/adjust tests in `dev/breeze/tests/test_selective_checks.py`.

skill_candidates.summary: One-time environment bootstrap: install prek, enable commit hooks, install the breeze shim per machine.
skill_candidates.evidence: Install prek: `uv tool install prek` - Enable commit hooks: `prek install` - Install breeze shim (one-time, per machine): `scripts/tools/setup_breeze` — installs `~/.local/bin/breeze` that runs breeze via `uvx` from the current git worktree's `dev/breeze`.

skill_candidates.summary: Tracking-issue workflow for workarounds/mitigations: open the issue first, reference it by number in the PR body, then link it as a comment at the workaround site in code.
skill_candidates.evidence: 1. Open the tracking issue first, before finalising the PR body. 2. Reference it in the PR body by number ... 3. Add a link to the tracking issue as a comment at the workaround itself, so the reference survives after the PR merges.

skill_candidates.summary: apache-magpie framework snapshot setup on a fresh clone via /magpie-setup before any framework skill (triage, code-review, stats, mentor) is usable.
skill_candidates.evidence: A fresh clone needs the snapshot populated before any framework skill is invocable. Run `/magpie-setup` (or follow `.claude/skills/magpie-setup/`) to fetch it per the committed `.apache-magpie.lock`.

always_on: Don't spell out "Directed Acyclic Graph" except for historical context.
Repository structure overview: UV workspace monorepo with key paths such as airflow-core, task-sdk, providers, airflow-ctl, chart, dev, and scripts.

Architecture boundaries: the Scheduler reads serialized Dags and never runs user code; Workers communicate only through the Task SDK and Execution API and never access the metadata DB directly; the Dag File Processor and Triggerer are steered through the Execution API by software guards that do not stop intentional bypass.
Security model: when flagging security concerns, distinguish actual vulnerabilities from documented known limitations and from deployment hardening opportunities, per the security model doc.

Shared libraries are separate small Python distributions under shared/, symlinked into the distributions that use them; internal tests live in the shared distribution, consumer tests live in the consuming distribution.
Imports go at the top of the file; valid exceptions are circular imports, lazy loading for worker isolation, and TYPE_CHECKING blocks.
Comment sparingly — code says what, comments say why; avoid narrating comments and don't repeat the same rationale at multiple sites.
Translate domain-layer exceptions to HTTPException at FastAPI route boundaries in airflow-core/src/airflow/core_api/ route handlers, using the right status code.
Bulk DELETE/UPDATE in the scheduler loop or any synchronous interval task must be batched with LIMIT and committed between batches, with indexed filter columns.
Name functions and methods with action verbs (get_, extract_, find_, compute_, build_); avoid noun-only names, except predicates (is_, has_).
SQLite is the default backend; use --backend postgres or --backend mysql for integration tests that need those databases; run docker network prune if Docker networking fails.
Put any files you generate (PR reviews, reports, scratch output) under files/, creating it if it doesn't exist.
Place temporary scripts in dev/ (mounted as /opt/airflow/dev/ inside Breeze).
For PR code review, prefer the magpie-pr-management-code-review skill (inline, per-finding accept/skip comments) over an ad-hoc review pass or a generic review command.

Target exactly 100% coverage of what the PR changes — every changed/added behaviour needs a test that fails without the change; don't test pre-existing logic or standard-library/third-party functions.
Use spec/autospec when mocking; prefer @mock.patch decorators over context managers for patching; use conf_vars for Airflow config overrides.
